hotspots.location: nonlop_ylm: per-type/per-atom-block main loop calling opernla_ylm/opernla_ylm_mv (projection <p|c>) (around 'call opernla_ylm' / 'call opernla_ylm_mv')
hotspots.reason: Dominant O(npw * nlmn * nincat * nspinor) work: builds projector overlaps using plane-wave loops and phase factors; high arithmetic plus heavy streaming of vectin/ffnlin/ph3din. Called for each atom block and type; often repeated for many bands/k-points in outer code.

hotspots.location: nonlop_ylm: per-type/per-atom-block call opernlb_ylm/opernlb_ylm_mv (apply Vnl to vector, build vectout/svectout) (around 'call opernlb_ylm' / 'call opernlb_ylm_mv')
hotspots.reason: Second dominant O(npwout * nlmn * nincat * nspinor) kernel; similar data movement intensity as projection, plus scatter/gather into vectout/svectout. Typically comparable to opernla cost when signs=2.

hotspots.location: nonlop_ylm: per-type/per-atom-block call opernlc_ylm (contraction with Dij/Sij, build gxfac and derivative arrays) (around 'call opernlc_ylm')
hotspots.reason: Dense small-matrix contractions over nlmn (and spinor channels) for each atom in block; can be compute-heavy for PAW (paw_opt>0) and for derivative choices (ndgxdt/nd2gxdt). Also creates additional working sets (gxfac*, dgxdtfac*, d2gxdtfac*).

hotspots.location: nonlop_ylm: repeated allocations/initializations inside ia3..ia4 atom-block loop (ABI_MALLOC + zeroing gx/gxfac/dgxdt/...)
hotspots.reason: Multiple large temporary arrays allocated and memset-to-zero for every atom block and type; overhead grows with many small blocks (mincat) and with derivative-heavy choices (large nd2gxdt). Can become significant when npw is moderate but many calls/bands.

hotspots.location: nonlop_ylm: ph1d3d calls for in/out phase factors when nloalg(2)<=0 (around 'call ph1d3d(...)')
hotspots.reason: Phase-factor generation scales with npw * nincat and is invoked for both kgin and kgout paths; can be a sizable fraction if phases are recomputed frequently (nloalg(2)<=0) and/or if opernl kernels are optimized.

hotspots.location: nonlop_ylm: MPI reductions at end for signs==1 (xmpi_sum on enlout/enlk/fnlk/strnlk/ddkk)
hotspots.reason: Collective reductions on comm_spinor; usually small compared to kernels but can dominate at scale (many ranks, small per-rank work) or if called very frequently (per band/k-point).

bottleneck_type.reasoning: The dominant kernels (opernla/opernlb) stream large plane-wave arrays (vectin/vectout, ffnl*, ph3d*, kpg*) with relatively low reuse, making performance sensitive to memory bandwidth and cache behavior. Additional pressure comes from frequent allocation/zeroing of large temporaries per atom block. Compute-heavy parts exist (Dij contractions), but overall arithmetic intensity is typically limited by moving plane-wave data.

optimization_suggestions.suggestion: Increase arithmetic intensity and vectorization by batching bands (ndat) and using GEMM-like formulations (projector matrix * wavefunction block) instead of repeated GEMV/scalar loops
optimization_suggestions.details: Restructure projection to compute <p|C> for multiple bands at once: build a (npw x nproj) projector matrix (or its factored representation) per atom-block/type and multiply by a (npw x nbatch) wavefunction block using BLAS-3 (DGEMM/ZGEMM) or vendor batched GEMM. Keep projector data in cache/packed format; align/contiguize ffnlin_typ and ph3din access. Prefer mv-dgemv only when nbatch=1; otherwise GEMM.

optimization_suggestions.suggestion: Fuse contraction+application where possible to reduce intermediate arrays (gxfac*, dgxdtfac*) and memory traffic
optimization_suggestions.expected_speedup: 1.2x-1.8x overall (signs=2 paths), higher for derivative-heavy choices
optimization_suggestions.details: Instead of materializing gxfac/gxfac_sij and derivative-factor arrays for all projectors/atoms, stream: compute gx for an atom (or small tile of projectors), apply Dij/Sij immediately, and accumulate into vectout/svectout. This reduces writes/reads of large temporaries and improves cache locality. Requires refactoring opernlc+opernlb interfaces or adding a combined kernel.

optimization_suggestions.suggestion: Preallocate and reuse work buffers across ia3..ia4 blocks and across calls; replace full zeroing with targeted initialization
optimization_suggestions.expected_speedup: 1.05x-1.3x overall (can be larger when many small blocks or large nd2gxdt)
optimization_suggestions.details: Move ABI_MALLOC/ABI_FREE of gx/gxfac/dgxdt/... outside the ia3 loop: allocate to maximum needed sizes (max nincat=mincat, max ndgxdt/nd2gxdt for current choice) and reuse. Use separate 'valid range' indices rather than clearing full arrays; when zero is required, use memset-like bulk operations once per reuse or only clear the slices that will be accumulated into. Consider a workspace object cached per thread/MPI rank.

optimization_suggestions.suggestion: Cache and reuse ph3d for k-points and atom blocks; avoid recomputation by setting nloalg(2)>0 or introducing persistent phase storage keyed by (kpt, atom-block, npw-set)
optimization_suggestions.expected_speedup: 1.05x-1.2x overall (more if phases currently recomputed often)
optimization_suggestions.details: If memory allows, store ph3din/ph3dout for all atom blocks (or for all atoms) and reuse across repeated nonlop_ylm calls for different bands at same k-point. Alternatively compute ph3d once per SCF iteration and pass in with nloalg(2)>0. Ensure ph3d arrays are contiguous and aligned for vector loads in opernl kernels.

optimization_suggestions.suggestion: Exploit symmetry/structure of Dij/Sij and use packed/blocked small-matrix kernels; consider precomputing per-type packed Dij and using batched SYRK/HEMM-like operations
optimization_suggestions.details: Dij/Sij are Hermitian and stored in triangular form; use specialized kernels that operate on packed triangular storage without expanding. Block over nlmn and atoms (nincat) to improve cache reuse. For PAW, prepack sij_typ/enl slices per type into SoA layout to reduce stride and branch overhead.

optimization_suggestions.suggestion: Reduce reduction frequency and overlap communication with computation
optimization_suggestions.details: Accumulate enlout/enlk/fnlk/strnlk/ddkk over multiple bands before calling xmpi_sum (if algorithmically allowed). Use nonblocking collectives (MPI_Iallreduce) to overlap with subsequent k-point/band work. Ensure reductions are on minimal necessary arrays (avoid reducing zeros for choices that do not use them).

gpu_suitability.reasoning: The dominant work is large, regular linear-algebra-like operations over plane waves and projectors (projection and application), which map well to GPUs when expressed as batched GEMM/GEMV and when phase/projector data are kept resident on device. Main challenges are many small atom blocks (mincat), frequent temporary allocations, and complex control flow for many 'choice' derivative modes.
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Port opernla_ylm/opernlb_ylm (and ideally fused opernlc+opernlb) to GPU using CUDA/HIP/oneAPI with BLAS-3 (cublas/rocblas) for band-batched GEMM. Keep vectin/vectout, ffnl*, ph3d*, kpg* on device; precompute/copy ph3d once per k-point. Use batched kernels over atom blocks and spinor components; avoid device malloc in inner loops by using persistent workspaces. For derivative modes, treat ndgxdt/nd2gxdt as extra small dimensions and fuse them into the batch dimension to increase occupancy.
